title: Handroanthus obscurus
taxonomy: Handroanthus obscurus is a member of the Bignoniaceae family, commonly known as the bignonia family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas. Handroanthus obscurus is a species of flowering plant in the Bignoniaceae family. The Bignoniaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Handroanthus obscurus being part of the tribe Tecomeae. The classification of Handroanthus obscurus is based on its morphological characteristics, molecular phylogenetics, and other taxonomic features.

intro: Handroanthus obscurus, also known as Ipe or Brazil rosewood, is a large, deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Handroanthus obscurus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as ipeacanol and isobutylamine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Handroanthus obscurus is commonly prepared as a bark or root decoction, as well as a powder or capsule supplement, to harness its health benefits and support overall well-being.
